news.summary: Pi Network, launched in 2019, attracted millions with its mobile mining app, accumulating over 70 million users worldwide. However, issues arose as the mainnet launch was delayed until February 2025, causing balance migration problems and price fluctuations. Initially trading at $2.98, Pi's value plummeted to $0.58 by May 2025, losing over 70%. Questions regarding Pi's transparency, decentralization, lack of utility, and exchange listings arose. Critics suggest Pi's structure resembles a multi-level marketing scheme and question its legitimacy. Pi's future hinges on transparency, utility integration, broader exchange availability, and true decentralization. Without significant changes, Pi Network risks fading into obscurity amidst skepticism.
news.headline: Is Pi Network dying? Initially promising easy mobile mining, it now faces challenges. With 70M users, its $2.98 token crumbled to $0.58 by May 2025. Controversies around transparency, decentralized governance, and utility continue. Can Pi bounce back amid skepticism?

news.summary: Microsoft Corporation co-founder and billionaire philanthropist, Bill Gates, recently criticized Tesla CEO Elon Musk for actions he believes are harming the world's poorest children. Gates expressed concerns in an interview with the Financial Times regarding Musk's decision to significantly reduce funding for the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) in February. The cuts have led to the expiration of life-saving food and medicines in warehouses, potentially causing a resurgence of diseases like measles, HIV, and polio. Gates, once the world's richest man, highlighted the impact of these abrupt cuts on vulnerable populations. Musk and the White House did not immediately respond to Benzinga's request for comment. Gates remarked, "The picture of the world's richest man killing the world's poorest children is not a pretty one." This criticism raises questions about the implications of reducing aid to critical global health programs.
news.headline: Bill Gates criticizes Elon Musk for cutting USAID funding, warning of life-saving aid expiration and disease resurgence.

news.summary: Match Group Inc. (NASDAQ: MTCH), the parent company of popular dating apps Tinder, Hinge, and Match.com, fell short of estimates in its first quarter earnings report. The new CEO Spencer Rascoff disclosed a restructuring plan during the earnings call, focusing on streamlining the company's array of brands, implementing artificial intelligence across its platforms, and emphasizing product innovation to drive growth. Despite these initiatives, the company also revealed intentions to cut its workforce by 13% and reduce operating expenses. Rascoff highlighted the integration of AI with Tinder to appeal to a younger audience, introducing features like "Double Date" for users to team up with friends. This strategic overhaul aims to enhance the company's position in the competitive dating app market.
news.headline: Match Group Inc. cuts 13% of workforce after earnings miss, focuses on Gen Z features like 'Double Date' on Tinder.

news.summary: Yesterday, on May 8, 2025, the Beijing Week at Expo 2025 Osaka kicked off in Beijing. The Beijing Central Axis Exhibition was the highlight of the event, showcasing the cultural heritage of the ancient capital. Divided into three zones - Panoramic Axis, Digital Axis, and Cultural Axis - the exhibition provides visitors with a comprehensive insight into the historical significance of Beijing. The event is expected to attract a significant number of visitors during its run.
news.headline: Beijing Central Axis Exhibition opens at Expo 2025 Osaka during Beijing Week, showcasing ancient capital culture.

news.summary: D-Wave Quantum Inc. reported record earnings, with CEO Alan Baratz showcasing advancements in energy-efficient blockchain hashing using quantum computing. A recent research paper, Blockchain with Proof of Quantum Work, demonstrated the ability of quantum computation to create and verify blockchain hashes, serving as unique identifiers for data to detect alterations. Baratz emphasized the potential of utilizing quantum computation for hashing and proof of work, estimating a reduction in electricity usage by up to 1000 times compared to traditional methods. By integrating quantum capabilities into blockchain computation, security and efficiency can be significantly enhanced. D-Wave implemented this blockchain architecture on four cloud-based annealing quantum systems.
news.headline: D-Wave Quantum Inc. CEO says quantum computing can enhance blockchain hashing's energy efficiency, requiring a fraction of the electricity used by classical resources.

news.summary: Before the markets open on Friday, May 9, Gogo Inc. (NASDAQ: GOGO) will be announcing its first-quarter earnings. Analysts are predicting earnings of 8 cents per share for the Broomfield, Colorado company, a decrease from the 16 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be $214.44 million, up from $104.32 million a year ago. Oakleigh Thorne was recently named Executive Chairman of Gogo. The company's stock closed at $7.57 on Thursday, reflecting a 0.5% increase. For further details and analyst ratings, interested readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page.
news.headline: Gogo to report Q1 earnings on May 9, expected at 8 cents per share, revenue at $214.44 million. Shares closed at $7.57.

news.summary: Essent Group Ltd (NYSE:ESNT) is set to announce its first quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, May 9. Analysts are predicting earnings of $1.65 per share for the Bermuda-based company, a decrease from $1.70 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be $310.79 million, up from $298.36 million in the previous year. In the fourth quarter, Essent Group reported weaker-than-expected earnings. The company's stock closed at $58.72 on Thursday, marking a 0.6% increase. For more information, readers can access analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga, sorting by various criteria.
news.headline: Wall Street analysts adjust Essent Group Ltd. expectations before Q1 earnings, projecting earnings at $1.65/share and revenue at $310.79 million; shares rose 0.6% to close at $58.72.

news.summary: The CEO of MARA Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: MARA), Fred Thiel, reaffirmed the company's dedication to its Bitcoin holding strategy during the recent first quarter earnings call. Thiel highlighted the advantages for stockholders, emphasizing their unique position in the market. He described their approach as a "twin turbocharge strategy," combining Bitcoin HODL play with mining activities for maximal benefits. Thiel explained that the shift to a "full HODL" strategy was informed by thorough analysis of market conditions and macroeconomic factors. The company aims to capitalize on the potential growth opportunities in the cryptocurrency market for the benefit of its shareholders.
news.headline: MARA Holdings Inc. CEO highlights 'twin turbocharge strategy' of Bitcoin HODL and mining, emphasizing benefits for stockholders.

news.summary: The European Union (EU) is taking a strong stance against the United States' tariff policies by planning to address the issue at the World Trade Organization (WTO). They are set to challenge the US's "reciprocal" tariff policies and duties on cars and car parts, with proposed countermeasures amounting to €95 billion ($107.4 billion). The European Commission has initiated a public consultation on these countermeasures, which target a wide range of US imports including agricultural and industrial goods, as well as alcoholic beverages such as bourbon and tequila. This move is part of ongoing negotiations to prevent a potential 20% reciprocal tariff on all imports from the United States.
news.headline: EU plans to challenge US tariffs at WTO, proposes countermeasures worth $107 billion; includes alcoholic beverages.

news.summary: A new report reveals that the global economy is projected to experience a growth rate of 4.9% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. This forecast is supported by an expected rebound in various sectors, with emerging markets and developing economies set to grow by 6%. However, the economic recovery remains uneven, with low-income countries facing greater challenges. The report highlights the importance of policy support and international cooperation to ensure a more inclusive and sustainable recovery. Additionally, it emphasizes the need for continued efforts to address income inequality and climate change in order to build a more resilient global economy.
news.headline: Nordic leaders discuss potential ceasefire with Trump and Zelenskiy.

news.summary: A recent report indicates a significant rise in global temperatures, with an average increase of 1.2 degrees Celsius compared to pre-industrial levels. This increase surpasses the limits set by the Paris Agreement, which aimed to keep the global temperature rise below 1.5 degrees Celsius. Additionally, the report highlights a concerning trend of unprecedented weather events, such as heatwaves and wildfires, across various regions. The need for urgent action is emphasized, with experts warning that without immediate measures, the global temperature could rise by 2.7 degrees Celsius by the end of the century. This underscores the crucial importance of reducing greenhouse gas emissions and implementing sustainable practices to mitigate the impact of climate change.
news.headline: Gold prices rise due to US-UK deal impact on market. - Source: Bloomberg.

news.summary: Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on May 9. Analysts predict the company, based in Houston, Texas, will post earnings of 45 cents per share, an increase from 41 cents per share a year ago. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $14 billion, up from $11.99 billion in the previous year. Harry Pefanis will retire as President of Plains by June 1, as announced on March 26. Shares of Plains All American Pipeline closed at $16.94 on Thursday, up by 1.4%. For more details and analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's website.
news.headline: Plains All American Pipeline (NASDAQ: PAA) to report Q1 earnings at $0.45/share, revenue at $14B on May 9. President to retire. Stock rose 1.4% to $16.94.

news.summary: Today, in Stockholm, Essity's Board of Directors announced the appointment of Ulrika Kolsrud as the new President and CEO. Kolsrud, who currently serves as President of Essity's Health & Medical division, is set to take on her new role starting June 1, 2025. This decision marks a significant change in leadership for the company.
news.headline: Ulrika Kolsrud named President and CEO of Essity in Stockholm. She will start on June 1, 2025, after serving as President of Health & Medical unit.
